picks.nrfi_analysis: NRFI prob 74.0% vs YRFI 26.0%. Book price +116 (source: book) — implied prob 46.3%. Avila NRFI score 1.589 (elite), Patrick NRFI score 1.119 (solid). Both SPs K/9 above 8.5 (10.29 and 9.59). BB/9 slightly above 3.0 for both (3.86 and 3.02) — mild caveat but model already incorporates. Park factor 0.97 < 1.05. EV = 74% win prob vs 46.3% implied = +27.7% EV. Qualifies on EV threshold of 5%+.
picks.key_edge: Book offering +116 on a 74% NRFI probability — massive market inefficiency. Fair price is -285; getting +116 represents ~28% positive EV.
picks.rationale: The book is pricing NRFI at +116 (46.3% implied) against a model probability of 74% — a 27.7% EV gap that is the single largest edge on the slate. Both starters carry K/9 above 9.5, Avila's 1.29 ERA reflects dominant SP quality that suppresses first-inning scoring. Pitcher-friendly Kauffman (runs factor 0.97) and elite NRFI scores for both pitchers (1.589 home, 1.119 away) reinforce the model. Tier C reflects limited confirmable data points (SMALL SAMPLE flags, missing MIL OPS, no home/away splits) despite the overwhelming EV — use fixed half-unit sizing.
